samples.target_compile_errors:
error[E0277]: `main` has invalid return type `i32`
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/restrict_def_2/sample_001/target.rs:19:14
   |
19 | fn main() -> i32 {
   |              ^^^ `main` can only return types that implement `Termination`
   |
   = help: consider using `()`, or a `Result`

error[E0502]: cannot borrow `buf` as immutable because it is also borrowed as mutable
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/restrict_def_2/sample_001/target.rs:33:18
   |
32 |         let p = &mut buf[0..n];
   |                      --- mutable borrow occurs here
33 |         let q = &buf[off..off + n];
   |                  ^^^ immutable borrow occurs here
34 |
35 |         f(n, p, q);
   |              - mutable borrow later used here
   |
   = help: use `.split_at_mut(position)` to obtain two mutable non-overlapping sub-slices

error[E0502]: cannot borrow `buf` as immutable because it is also borrowed as mutable
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/restrict_def_2/sample_001/target.rs:59:18
   |
58 |         let p = &mut buf[off..off + n];
   |                      --- mutable borrow occurs here
59 |         let q = &buf[0..n];
   |                  ^^^ immutable borrow occurs here
60 |
61 |         f(n, p, q);
   |              - mutable borrow later used here
   |
   = help: use `.split_at_mut(position)` to obtain two mutable non-overlapping sub-slices
